questions.text: Health is defined as a state of complete ________ well-being.
questions.explanation: According to WHO, health is not just the absence of disease but total physical, mental, and social well-being.

questions.text: Which of the following is a bacterial disease?
questions.explanation: Typhoid is caused by Salmonella typhi, a bacterium.

questions.text: Widal test is used for the diagnosis of:
questions.explanation: The Widal test detects antibodies against the Salmonella bacteria causing typhoid fever.

questions.text: Common cold is caused by:
questions.explanation: Rhino viruses infect the nose and respiratory passage but not the lungs.

questions.text: Malaria is transmitted by the bite of:
questions.explanation: The female Anopheles mosquito acts as the vector for the Plasmodium parasite.

questions.text: The infectious stage of Plasmodium for humans is:
questions.explanation: Sporozoites are injected into the human blood when an infected mosquito bites.

questions.text: The malignant malaria is caused by:
questions.explanation: Plasmodium falciparum causes the most serious and often fatal type of malaria.

questions.text: Amoebic dysentery (Amoebiasis) is caused by:
questions.explanation: It is a protozoan parasite found in the large intestine of humans.

questions.text: Elephantiasis (Filariasis) is caused by:
questions.explanation: The filarial worms cause chronic inflammation of the lymphatic vessels, usually of lower limbs.

questions.text: Ringworm is a ________ disease.
questions.explanation: Fungi belonging to genera Microsporum, Trichophyton, and Epidermophyton cause ringworm.

questions.text: The immunity present from birth is called:
questions.explanation: Innate immunity is non-specific and provides barriers to entry of foreign agents.

questions.text: Interferons are secreted by:
questions.explanation: Interferons protect non-infected cells from further viral infection.

questions.text: Which cells produce antibodies?
questions.explanation: B-cells produce an army of proteins (antibodies) into our blood to fight pathogens.

questions.text: The antibody found in colostrum (mother's milk) is:
questions.explanation: IgA provides passive immunity to the newborn baby.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of active immunity?
options: Immunity developed after recovering from an infection
questions.explanation: Active immunity is produced by the host's own body in response to antigens.

questions.text: Allergies are due to the release of chemicals like ________ from mast cells.
questions.explanation: These chemicals trigger the inflammatory response seen in allergies.

questions.text: AIDS is caused by HIV, which belongs to the group:
questions.explanation: Retroviruses have an RNA genome and use reverse transcriptase to replicate.

questions.text: HIV attacks which cells in the human body?
questions.explanation: Destruction of helper T-lymphocytes leads to a progressive decrease in immunity.

questions.text: The diagnostic test for AIDS is:
questions.explanation: Enzyme Linked Immuno-Sorbent Assay (ELISA) is a widely used screening test for HIV.

questions.text: The property of normal cells by which they inhibit the growth of other cells is:
questions.explanation: Cancer cells lose this property, leading to uncontrolled proliferation.

questions.text: The movement of cancer cells to distant sites through blood is called:
questions.explanation: Metastasis is the most feared property of malignant tumors.

questions.text: Opioids like Heroin are obtained from:
questions.explanation: Heroin is a semi-synthetic derivative obtained by acetylation of morphine from the poppy plant.

questions.text: Cannabinoids generally affect which system of the body?
questions.explanation: These drugs are known for their effects on the heart and blood vessels.

questions.text: Cocaine is obtained from 'Coca' plant, which is native to:
questions.explanation: Erythroxylum coca is native to South America and interferes with dopamine transport.

questions.text: Tobacco contains nicotine which stimulates the ________ gland.
questions.explanation: Nicotine triggers the release of adrenaline and nor-adrenaline, increasing blood pressure.

questions.text: Cirrhosis of the liver is caused by chronic intake of:
questions.explanation: Long-term alcohol abuse leads to severe liver damage and scarring.

questions.text: Which of the following is an autoimmune disease?
questions.explanation: In autoimmune diseases, the body's immune system attacks its own cells.

questions.text: MALT (Mucosal Associated Lymphoid Tissue) constitutes about ________ percent of lymphoid tissue in humans.
questions.explanation: MALT is found lining the major tracts (respiratory, digestive, urogenital).

questions.text: Which of the following is a primary lymphoid organ?
questions.explanation: Primary lymphoid organs (bone marrow and thymus) are where lymphocytes mature.

questions.text: In the structure of an antibody, how many light and heavy chains are present?
questions.explanation: An antibody molecule is represented as H2L2.

questions.text: Pneumonia is an infection of the:
questions.explanation: Alveoli get filled with fluid, leading to severe problems in respiration.

questions.text: Symptoms like internal bleeding, muscular pain, and fever after consuming contaminated water indicate:
questions.explanation: Ascaris lumbricoides is an intestinal parasite transmitted through contaminated water and vegetables.

questions.text: Haemozoin is a toxic substance released during ________ which causes chills.
questions.explanation: It is released when RBCs rupture during the life cycle of Plasmodium.

questions.text: The vector for Dengue and Chikungunya is:
questions.explanation: Aedes aegypti mosquitoes spread these viral diseases.

questions.text: Which immune response is responsible for graft rejection during organ transplant?
questions.explanation: T-lymphocytes are responsible for CMI, which recognizes and attacks non-self tissue.

questions.text: Cancer causing agents are called:
questions.explanation: Physical, chemical, or biological agents that transform normal cells into cancerous cells.

questions.text: NACO stands for:
questions.explanation: NACO and NGOs work to educate people about AIDS prevention.

questions.text: LSD is obtained from:
questions.explanation: Lysergic acid diethylamide (LSD) is a powerful hallucinogen.

questions.text: Biological response modifiers like ________ are given to cancer patients.
questions.explanation: These help the immune system in identifying and destroying the tumor cells.

questions.text: Withdrawal syndrome is characterized by:
options: Relief when drug use is resumed
questions.explanation: It occurs when a person suddenly stops using a substance they are addicted to.
